leftLateral.caption: Free Destrieux atlas · left lateral inflated surface

rightLateral.caption: Free Destrieux atlas · right lateral inflated surface

superior.caption: Free Destrieux atlas · superior inflated surface

inferior.caption: Free Destrieux atlas · inferior inflated surface

layers.name: Cortical Lobes
layers.description: Atlas-backed cortical lobes on a free fsaverage surface, using lateral, superior, and inferior study views.

regions.label: Frontal Lobe
regions.teachingSummary: Anterior cortex central to executive control, planning, voluntary movement, and speech production.

regions.label: Parietal Lobe
regions.teachingSummary: Dorsal posterior cortex for somatosensation, spatial attention, and sensorimotor integration.

regions.label: Temporal Lobe
regions.teachingSummary: Lateral and ventral cortex linked to hearing, language, memory, and object recognition.

regions.label: Occipital Lobe
regions.teachingSummary: Posterior cortex specialized for visual processing from early visual cortex to extrastriate regions.

layers.name: Surface Landmarks
layers.description: Selected atlas-backed gyri and sulci. Some prompts are easiest from superior or inferior views, so switching views is part of the challenge.

regions.label: Superior Frontal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Dorsal frontal gyrus contributing to higher-order control and motor planning.

regions.label: Middle Frontal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Lateral frontal cortex strongly associated with working memory and cognitive control.

regions.label: Inferior Frontal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Inferior frontal cortex that includes classic language-related cortex in the dominant hemisphere.

regions.label: Precentral Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Primary motor strip just anterior to the central sulcus.

regions.label: Postcentral Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Primary somatosensory strip just posterior to the central sulcus.

regions.label: Superior Parietal Lobule
regions.teachingSummary: Dorsal parietal cortex for visuospatial and sensorimotor integration.

regions.label: Supramarginal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Inferior parietal gyrus involved in phonology, praxis, and multimodal integration.

regions.label: Angular Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Posterior inferior parietal gyrus associated with language, semantic processing, and default-mode cognition.

regions.label: Superior Temporal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Upper temporal cortex important for auditory and speech-related processing.

regions.label: Middle Temporal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Temporal association cortex involved in semantic and visual processing.

regions.label: Inferior Temporal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Ventral temporal cortex important for higher-level visual object recognition.

regions.label: Fusiform Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Ventral temporal region linked to high-level visual recognition such as faces and words.

regions.label: Parahippocampal Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Medial ventral temporal cortex associated with memory and scene processing.

regions.label: Lateral Occipital Cortex
regions.teachingSummary: Lateral occipital surface that supports higher-order visual analysis.

regions.label: Cuneus
regions.teachingSummary: Dorsomedial occipital cortex adjacent to early visual cortex.

regions.label: Lingual Gyrus
regions.teachingSummary: Medial ventral occipital cortex associated with visual processing and reading-related functions.

regions.label: Insular Cortex
regions.teachingSummary: Deep cortex associated with interoception, salience, affect, and taste.

regions.label: Central Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Major sulcus separating frontal motor cortex from parietal somatosensory cortex.

regions.label: Precentral Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Sulcus just anterior to the precentral gyrus.

regions.label: Postcentral Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Sulcus just posterior to the postcentral gyrus.

regions.label: Lateral Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Sylvian fissure separating temporal cortex from frontal and parietal cortex.

regions.label: Superior Frontal Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Frontal sulcus bordering the superior frontal gyrus.

regions.label: Middle Frontal Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Frontal sulcus on the lateral convexity between middle frontal territories.

regions.label: Inferior Frontal Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Inferior frontal sulcus near the classic inferior frontal language-related cortex.

regions.label: Intraparietal Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Dorsal parietal sulcus involved in visuospatial attention and eye-hand coordination.

regions.label: Superior Temporal Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Temporal sulcus important in social perception, multisensory integration, and language processing.

regions.label: Inferior Temporal Sulcus
regions.teachingSummary: Ventral temporal sulcus bordering visual association cortex.

notes: Generated from Nilearn's free fsaverage5 surface and Destrieux sulco-gyral atlas.
This first surface pass focuses on outer cortical anatomy; medial and slice-based landmarks should still be taught in sagittal/coronal/axial layers.
